content.problemStatement:
An array BBB of size 2‚čÖM2 \cdot M2‚čÖM is called good if the following condition holds:


For each i=1,2,‚Ä¶,Mi = 1, 2, \ldots, Mi=1,2,‚Ä¶,M, B2‚čÖi‚ąí1=B2‚čÖiB_{2 \cdot i - 1} = B_{2 \cdot i}B2‚čÖi‚ąí1‚Äč=B2‚čÖi‚Äč.
For example, B=[1,1,3,3]B = [1, 1, 3, 3]B=[1,1,3,3] and B=[4,4]B = [4, 4]B=[4,4] are good, but B=[1,1,2]B = [1, 1, 2]B=[1,1,2] is not.

You are given an array AAA of length NNN. Find the length of longest subsequence of AAA which is good.

A subsequence is formed by deleting several elements from the array without changing the order of the remaining elements. Notably, a subsequence does not need to be contiguous.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, - the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the length of the longest good subsequence of AAA.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The entire array is good.
Test Case 2 : A subsequence [2,2][2, 2][2,2] of length 222 is good.
